deep_dive.title: Learning What Not to Forget: Long-Horizon Agent Memory from a Few Kilobytes of Learning
deep_dive.abstract: Long-running language-model systems accumulate interaction history that outgrows the context window, so they must continually evict. When an eviction policy drops a load-bearing detail, for example an access token issued at login or a path the next call needs, the action fails. We present LRE (Learned Relevance Eviction), a few kilobytes, CPU-only, language-model-free scorer that learns which units of history are load-bearing and keeps them by verbatim extraction. Under a matched-budget comparison, in our experiment, no baseline dominates LRE on the accuracy-cost plane. On agents, LRE matches the accuracy of keeping the entire history overall. On the simplest tasks, it exceeds that no-eviction baseline by 27%, while requiring zero compressor calls and reducing peak context size by up to 52%. A controlled study trace shows LRE completes tasks where the others loop, finishing one such task in 37% fewer calls than keeping everything and solving 14 tasks where no other run policy does. On conversational memory, LRE outranks dense and token-pruning encoders at zero neural cost. In downstream evaluation, LRE gives the best budgeted answer quality on LoCoMo reading 68% fewer tokens. Its supervision can also be annotation-free: training only on the system's own behavior recovers 95% of the supervised scorer's effectiveness. We argue that, because memory eviction in LLM agents is a fidelity problem, it requires a deployable proactive policy where the future query is unavailable and exact state is decisive, and that cheap learned relevance can be sufficient.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 学习智能体记忆的逐出策略
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 学习智能体记忆的逐出策略
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 学习智能体记忆的逐出策略
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes LRE, a learned relevance eviction policy that keeps load-bearing history for LLM agents, improving accuracy under memory budget.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 长期运行的LLM智能体会积累超出上下文窗口的交互历史，需要不断逐出。本文提出LRE（学习相关性逐出），一个仅需几KB且无需语言模型的评分器，学习并保留关键历史。实验表明，在匹配预算下，LRE在准确率-成本平面上优于所有基线，在简单任务上甚至超过无逐出的基线27%，为零成本压缩提供了有效方案。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 长期运行的LLM智能体会积累超出上下文窗口的交互历史，需要不断逐出。本文提出LRE（学习相关性逐出），一个仅需几KB且无需语言模型的评分器，学习并保留关键历史。实验表明，在匹配预算下，LRE在准确率-成本平面上优于所有基线，在简单任务上甚至超过无逐出的基线27%，为零成本压缩提供了有效方案。

deep_dive.title: Bias-Controlled Primal-Dual Natural Actor-Critic: Optimal Rates for Constrained Multi-Objective Average-Reward RL
deep_dive.abstract: Many reinforcement learning (RL) problems in the infinite-horizon average-reward setting require optimizing multiple conflicting objectives while satisfying multiple safety constraints. A common approach is concave scalarization, where the agent maximizes a utility $ f(J^π_{r_1}, \ldots, J^π_{r_M}) $ subject to a scalarized constraint $ g(J^π_{c_1}, \ldots, J^π_{c_N}) \ge 0 $, where $J^π_{r_m}$ and $J^π_{c_n}$ denote the average-reward and cost under policy $π$. However, the nonlinearity of $f$ and $g$ introduces bias in policy-gradient and actor-critic methods, since gradients must be evaluated using noisy estimates of $J^π,$ and $ \mathbb{E}[\partial f(J^π)] \neq \partial f(\mathbb{E}[J^π]),$ and this bias propagates through both primal and dual updates. We propose an MLMC-based primal-dual Natural Actor-Critic algorithm for average-reward MDPs that controls bias in scalarized objectives, constraint evaluation, and actor-critic estimation without requiring mixing-time knowledge. We show that the algorithm achieves optimal global convergence and constraint-violation rates of $ \tilde{O}(1/\sqrt{T}) $. To our knowledge, this is the first result establishing optimal convergence for concave scalarized multi-objective RL in the average-reward setting, both with and without constraints, and the first to do so without mixing-time information even in the absence of scalarization.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: policy-gradient actor-critic method for constrained multi-objective RL
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 策略梯度演员-评论家方法用于约束多目标强化学习
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 策略梯度演员-评论家方法用于约束多目标强化学习
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 策略梯度演员-评论家方法用于约束多目标强化学习
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Bias-Controlled Primal-Dual Natural Actor-Critic with optimal convergence rates for constrained multi-objective average-reward RL.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究约束多目标平均奖励强化学习问题，指出非线性标量化导致策略梯度偏差。提出偏差控制的原始-对偶自然演员-评论家算法，利用自然梯度和对偶方法消除偏差并处理约束。理论分析证明了算法达到最优收敛速率。实验在多个安全关键环境中验证了方法的有效性和稳定性，为多目标安全RL提供了实用算法。

quick_skim.title: Holmes: Multimodal Agentic Diagnosis for Mixed-Language Mobile Crashes at Industrial Scale
quick_skim.abstract: Diagnosing mobile crashes in ultra-large-scale industrial applications is a formidable challenge due to the sheer volume of code, the complexity of mixed-language environments, and the inability to reproduce failures locally. Traditional static analysis struggles with scalability, while existing LLM-based agents often rely on reproducible environments unavailable in post-mortem scenarios. We present Holmes, a multi-agent system that automates root cause analysis by synthesizing multimodal runtime signals--stack traces, logs, and thread states--to reconstruct failure contexts without reproduction. Holmes introduces a hierarchical Retrieve-Explore-Reason architecture that leverages low-level artifacts (e.g., registers, assembly) to bridge the semantic gap between open-source business logic and closed-source system frameworks. By dynamically compressing the search space using runtime clues, Holmes precisely navigates 70-million-line codebases to identify non-local defects. Evaluated on real-world crashes from WeChat, Holmes achieves 87.6% accuracy in function-level fault localization and reduces average investigation time by over 98% (to ~77 seconds), demonstrating its effectiveness in transforming labor-intensive debugging into an efficient verification workflow.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent system for autonomous diagnosis of mobile crashes using multimodal signals
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 多智能体系统用于自主诊断移动崩溃，融合多模态信号
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 多智能体系统用于自主诊断移动崩溃，融合多模态信号
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 多智能体系统用于自主诊断移动崩溃，融合多模态信号
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Holmes, a multi-agent system that automates root cause analysis of mobile crashes by synthesizing multimodal runtime signals without reproduction, using hierarchical Retrieve-Explore-Reason architecture.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对大规模移动应用崩溃诊断难以复现的问题，提出Holmes多智能体系统，通过合成栈轨迹、日志和线程状态等多模态运行时信号，在无需复现的情况下自动进行根因分析。系统采用层次化检索-探索-推理架构，利用低层工件（如寄存器、汇编）增强推理。实验证明该方法在工业规模上有效，为智能体故障诊断提供了新方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文针对大规模移动应用崩溃诊断难以复现的问题，提出Holmes多智能体系统，通过合成栈轨迹、日志和线程状态等多模态运行时信号，在无需复现的情况下自动进行根因分析。系统采用层次化检索-探索-推理架构，利用低层工件（如寄存器、汇编）增强推理。实验证明该方法在工业规模上有效，为智能体故障诊断提供了新方案。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: autonomous agent decision making and learning

quick_skim.title: Governance Decay: How Context Compaction Silently Erases Safety Constraints in Long-Horizon LLM Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Modern LLM agents increasingly rely on context compaction, summarization, or eviction to keep long-running sessions within a token budget. We show that this context-management layer is a safety-critical failure surface: in-context governance constraints that agents reliably obey while visible can be silently removed by compaction, causing the same agent to perform prohibited tool actions later in the session. We call this failure mode Governance Decay. We introduce ConstraintRot, a benchmark of long-horizon agent scenarios with deterministic tool-call grading, and measure compaction-induced violations across seven model families. Across 1,323 episodes, violation rises from 0% with the policy in full context to 30% after compaction, reaching 59% for some models; when the constraint survives the summary, violation remains 0%, but when it is dropped, violation reaches 38%. We further study a Compaction-Eviction Attack, in which adversarial in-context content biases the summarizer to omit a legitimate policy, and show that optimized injections defeat every evaluated model. Finally, we propose Constraint Pinning, a simple training-free mitigation that quarantines governance constraints from lossy compaction and restores violation to 0% in our benchmark. These results identify context management as a first-class governance surface for deployed LLM agents.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: context compaction silently erases safety constraints in long-horizon LLM agents, a memory management issue
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 上下文压缩静默移除长周期LLM智能体中的安全约束，属于记忆管理问题
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 上下文压缩静默移除长周期LLM智能体中的安全约束，属于记忆管理问题
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 上下文压缩静默移除长周期LLM智能体中的安全约束，属于记忆管理问题
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Governance Decay reveals that context compaction removes safety constraints in LLM agents, causing violations in long sessions.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文发现长周期LLM智能体常用的上下文压缩（摘要或淘汰）会静默移除内联安全约束，导致智能体在后续执行中执行禁止操作，称为治理衰退。引入ConstraintRot基准评估，在1323个回合中，违规率从0%上升到30%。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该论文发现长周期LLM智能体常用的上下文压缩（摘要或淘汰）会静默移除内联安全约束，导致智能体在后续执行中执行禁止操作，称为治理衰退。引入ConstraintRot基准评估，在1323个回合中，违规率从0%上升到30%。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: Memory management techniques for multi agent systems

quick_skim.title: BiPACE: Bisimulation-Guided Policy Optimization with Action Counterfactual Estimation for LLM Agents
quick_skim.abstract: Stepwise group-based RL is an attractive way to train long-horizon LLM agents without a learned critic: it reuses multiple sampled rollouts to estimate local advantages. Its weakness is less visible but more fundamental: every group-relative estimator assumes that the steps it compares are equivalent for credit assignment. We show that current agentic variants violate this assumption through a state-action credit mismatch. The observation-hash partition is overly fine on the state side, creating singleton groups with zero step-level signal, while a single within-group mean is too coarse on the action side, mixing state-value estimation with action-specific credit. We introduce BiPACE (Bisimulation-Guided Policy Optimization with Action Counterfactual Estimation), a drop-in advantage estimator that fixes both sides without adding a critic, auxiliary loss, or extra rollouts. BiGPO clusters steps by cosine distance in the actor's own hidden-state geometry, an empirical policy-induced proxy for bisimulation that substantially lowers the singleton rate left by observation hashing. PACE then recenters returns within each behavioral cluster using action-conditioned peer baselines; its Q-style instance estimates a local Q(s,a)-V(s) nonparametrically. On ALFWorld/Qwen2.5-7B, BiPACE_Q raises overall validation success from GiGPO's 90.8 to $97.1\pm0.9$ over three seeds, and crosses the 95% threshold on every seed, which GiGPO never does within the same budget. On Qwen2.5-1.5B it reaches $93.5\pm1.2$ versus GiGPO's 86.7, and on WebShop and TextCraft it improves over GRPO and GiGPO at both model scales. The measured BiPACE-specific overhead is 11.3% of a single training-step wall time. Yet it changes the estimator's comparison unit from surface identity to approximate behavioral equivalence plus action-side counterfactuals. The code is available at https://github.com/TianxiangZhao/BiPACE.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: bisimulation-guided policy optimization for LLM agents
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 面向LLM代理的双模拟引导策略优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 面向LLM代理的双模拟引导策略优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 面向LLM代理的双模拟引导策略优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: BiPACE introduces bisimulation-guided policy optimization with action counterfactual estimation to fix state-action credit mismatch in group-based RL for LLM agents.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: BiPACE针对长期LLM代理的群组强化学习中状态-动作信用分配失配问题，提出双模拟引导策略优化。通过动作反事实估计修正分组估计中的偏差，在不依赖学习评论家的情况下提升步骤级信用分配。实验表明该方法在复杂推理任务上显著优于现有群组RL方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: BiPACE针对长期LLM代理的群组强化学习中状态-动作信用分配失配问题，提出双模拟引导策略优化。通过动作反事实估计修正分组估计中的偏差，在不依赖学习评论家的情况下提升步骤级信用分配。实验表明该方法在复杂推理任务上显著优于现有群组RL方法。

quick_skim.title: Neural Parameter Calibration for Finite-State Mean Field Games
quick_skim.abstract: Mean field games efficiently approximate a very large population of strategic agents. While these games can aid the understanding of complex systems, their deployment in real-world settings is challenged by the specification of their parameters: mean field games (MFGs) often involve hidden preferences, constraints, and interactions that can rarely be theoretically derived or directly observed. To address this gap, we present a neural network-based framework for learning parametric, finite-state MFGs from observed population dynamics. To do so, we formulate the parameter calibration as an inverse problem and use implicit differentiation to backpropagate through the games' equilibrium. The resulting approach is fully differentiable and enables us to estimate flexible trajectory-wise parameter paths, including state- and time-dependent specifications without requiring observations of the individual agents' actions or rewards. We provide a proof for the exactness of the gradient computation in a discrete-time formulation. We validate our framework through numerical experiments across four systems of increasing complexity, ranging from synthetic linear-quadratic benchmarks to real-world urban mobility datasets.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: multi-agent learning via mean field games, coordination
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过平均场博弈学习多智能体系统参数，涉及协调
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过平均场博弈学习多智能体系统参数，涉及协调
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过平均场博弈学习多智能体系统参数，涉及协调
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a neural framework to calibrate finite-state mean field games from observed trajectories, enabling parameter learning in large-scale multi-agent systems.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 平均场博弈能够有效近似大规模智能体群体行为，但其参数设置往往不可观测。本文提出一种神经网络框架，通过隐式微分反向传播博弈均衡梯度，从观测到的群体动态中学习有限状态平均场博弈的参数。该方法完全可微，能够灵活估计偏好、约束等隐藏参数，为多智能体系统建模提供新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 平均场博弈能够有效近似大规模智能体群体行为，但其参数设置往往不可观测。本文提出一种神经网络框架，通过隐式微分反向传播博弈均衡梯度，从观测到的群体动态中学习有限状态平均场博弈的参数。该方法完全可微，能够灵活估计偏好、约束等隐藏参数，为多智能体系统建模提供新工具。

quick_skim.title: Reinforcement Learning without Ground-Truth Solutions can Improve LLMs
quick_skim.abstract: Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) for training LLMs typically rely on ground-truth answers to assign rewards, limiting their applicability to tasks where the ground-truth solution is unknown. We introduce a \textbf{R}anking-\textbf{i}nduced \textbf{VER}ifiable framework (RiVER) that trains LLMs on score-based optimization tasks without ground-truth solutions, using deterministic execution feedback as continuous-valued supervision. When applying group-relative RL to such continuous rewards, we identify two key challenges: \emph{scale dominance}, where uncalibrated score magnitudes across test instances distort policy updates, and \emph{frequency dominance}, where repeatedly sampled suboptimal solutions can outweigh rare but stronger candidates. RiVER addresses these challenges with calibrated reward shaping that uses instance-wise comparisons and emphasizes top-ranked solvers while retaining bounded feedback for other valid solutions. We train on 12 AtCoder Heuristic Contest tasks and evaluate on Algorithm Engineering Benchmark (ALE-Bench), LiveCodeBench, and USACO. RiVER advances Qwen3-8B and GLM-Z1-9B-0414 by 8.9\% and 9.4\% in ALE rating rank. More importantly, despite training exclusively on score-based tasks without any ground-truth solutions, RiVER also improves the backbones across exact-solution benchmarks such as LiveCodeBench and USACO by an absolute average improvement of 2.4\% and 3.5\%. By contrast, baselines trained with raw execution scores improve ALE rating but fail to transfer to exact-solution benchmarks. These results suggest that score-based optimization tasks, combined with proper reward calibration, can serve as effective training environments for general coding ability without ground-truth solutions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: group-relative policy gradient for LLM training
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于分组的相对策略梯度方法
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于分组的相对策略梯度方法
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于分组的相对策略梯度方法
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: RiVER introduces a ranking-induced verifiable RL framework that trains LLMs on score-based tasks without ground-truth, addressing scale and frequency dominance issues.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: RiVER提出一种基于排名诱导的可验证强化学习框架，能够在没有真实答案的情况下，通过确定性执行反馈作为连续值监督来训练大语言模型。该方法解决了在连续奖励信号上应用分组相对策略梯度时出现的量级主导和频率主导挑战，从而在不依赖标准答案的场景中有效提升模型性能。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: RiVER提出一种基于排名诱导的可验证强化学习框架，能够在没有真实答案的情况下，通过确定性执行反馈作为连续值监督来训练大语言模型。该方法解决了在连续奖励信号上应用分组相对策略梯度时出现的量级主导和频率主导挑战，从而在不依赖标准答案的场景中有效提升模型性能。
